behavior_summary: This script implements a simple "U8P_WALLSWIT::look" examine handler and a richer "U8P_WALLSWIT::use" handler for a wall-mounted switch item.
U8P_WALLSWIT::look: when the player inspects the object it emits a short bark text "switch" and then suspends/ends the U8P_WALLSWIT::look process.
U8P_WALLSWIT::use: reads the item's current visual frame (local3) and quality/state (local4) and branches on those values to produce different in-game effects:

- For certain frame values the handler increments or decrements the item's visual frame, plays a SFX, spawns a short-lived helper process (spawn -> FREE::func1C19) and suspends to allow the change/effect to complete.
- For quality 0 and 1 the handler plays a SFX, toggles Camera::invertScreen on/off and flips the item's quality between 0 and 1.

- For quality 2 and 3 the handler chooses branch-specific resources/SFX and may spawn calls to activate or deactivate a linked SLIDER object (if a referenced object local2 exists and its quality matches local4).
The overall purpose is to provide player feedback (audio/visual), change the switch's appearance/state, and coordinate with other world objects (sliders/Camera) when appropriate.
notable_control_flow: U8P_WALLSWIT::use branches heavily on two runtime reads: Item::getFrame(this) (local3) and Item::getQuality(this) (local4) to select distinct behaviors.
Several branches perform an immediate Item::setFrame(...) change, then spawn a helper and suspend, implying a visible state change with a short-lived effect or delay.
Quality 0/1 branch toggles a global visual effect via Camera::invertScreen and flips the item's quality (state toggle).

Quality 2/3 branches appear to coordinate with another object (local2) by conditionally spawning SLIDER::activateSlider or SLIDER::deactivateSlider when the linked object's quality matches the expected value.
Numeric literals and specific spawn targets are present and used to select SFX or spawn targets; their precise in-game resource mappings are not explicit in the decompilation.
open_questions: What exact in-game resources / sound meanings correspond to the numeric SFX codes used in playSFX calls (concrete audio feedback mapping unknown).
What object is stored in local2 (source/origin of the linked SLIDER reference) and how it is assigned in the larger level data—its origin is not explicit in this snippet.
Precise semantics of the numeric 'frame' values and their mapping to switch visuals/animations (which frames increment/decrement and why) are inferred but not explicit.
Exact spawn target and effect of spawn -> FREE::func1C19 (helper) are unclear; it appears to be a transient effect or delay but its implementation is unknown.
Behavioral differences between quality 2 and 3 beyond selecting different resources/offsets are present but not fully detailed in this decompilation.

main_function.name: U8P_WALLSWIT::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the in-world short description when the player examines the wall switch (display the word 'switch').
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player 'looks at' or examines the WALLSWIT item (U8P_WALLSWIT::look/examine action).
Acts as a U8P_WALLSWIT::look-type process (process type 0x0208) and ends after emitting the bark.

function_map.name: U8P_WALLSWIT::look
function_map.role: examine/description handler that displays a short bark and then suspends

function_map.name: U8P_WALLSWIT::use
function_map.role: utility/U8P_WALLSWIT::use handler: changes item frame/quality, plays SFX, toggles Camera invert, and coordinates with linked SLIDER objects; spawns helper tasks as needed

external_calls.purpose: Read the switch's current visual frame/index to decide whether to increment or decrement its display.
Read the switch's quality/state selector (values 0..3 observed) to pick branch behavior (invert, slider activation, alternate resources).
Apply a computed visual frame change (+1 or -1) to the item for immediate feedback.
Change the item's quality/state (used to flip between 0 and 1 in invert-screen branches and to set local flags in other branches).
Play audio feedback for switch actions; different numeric codes are used per branch to provide distinct sounds.
Spawn a short-lived helper/cleanup process after some frame-change branches (likely to animate or delay post-toggle behavior).
Enable or disable a screen inversion effect when toggling between quality 0 and 1.
When a linked slider object (local2) exists and matches expected quality, spawn a call to activate the slider (start movement/enable behavior).
When conditions indicate deactivation, spawn a call to deactivate the linked slider (stop/reverse behavior).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements a simple "U8P_WALLSWIT::look" examine handler and a richer "U8P_WALLSWIT::use" handler for a wall-mounted switch item.
